title: An explicit operator explains end-to-end computation in the modern neural networks used for sequence and language modeling
summary: We establish a mathematical correspondence between state space models, a state-of-the-art architecture for capturing long-range dependencies in data, and an exactly solvable nonlinear oscillator network. As a specific example of this general correspondence, we analyze the diagonal linear time-invariant implementation of the Structured State Space Sequence model (S4). The correspondence embeds S4D, a specific implementation of S4, into a ring network topology, in which recent inputs are encoded, as waves of activity traveling over the one-dimensional spatial layout of the network. We then de...

metadata.comment: Derives an explicit operator for the full forward pass of S4D, giving an analytical input-output description of modern state-space sequence models.

title: On Bayesian Softmax-Gated Mixture-of-Experts Models
summary: Mixture-of-experts models provide a flexible framework for learning complex probabilistic input-output relationships by combining multiple expert models through an input-dependent gating mechanism. These models have become increasingly prominent in modern machine learning, yet their theoretical properties in the Bayesian framework remain largely unexplored. In this paper, we study Bayesian mixture-of-experts models, focusing on the ubiquitous softmax-based gating mechanism. Specifically, we investigate the asymptotic behavior of the posterior distribution for three fundamental statistical t...

metadata.comment: Provides one of the first systematic Bayesian theories for softmax-gated mixture-of-experts, including contraction, estimation, and model selection.

title: From Signal Degradation to Computation Collapse: Uncovering the Two Failure Modes of LLM Quantization
summary: Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) is critical for the efficient deployment of Large Language Models (LLMs). While 4-bit quantization is widely regarded as an optimal trade-off, reducing the precision to 2-bit usually triggers a catastrophic ``performance cliff.'' It remains unclear whether the underlying mechanisms differ fundamentally. Consequently, we conduct a systematic mechanistic analysis, revealing two qualitatively distinct failure modes: Signal Degradation, where the computational patterns remain intact but information precision is impaired by cumulative error; and Computation Colla...

metadata.comment: Identifies two distinct failure modes in LLM post-training quantization—signal degradation versus computation collapse—and tests mechanism-aware repairs.

title: Rethinking Intrinsic Dimension Estimation in Neural Representations
summary: The analysis of neural representation has become an integral part of research aiming to better understand the inner workings of neural networks. While there are many different approaches to investigate neural representations, an important line of research has focused on doing so through the lens of intrinsic dimensions (IDs). Although this perspective has provided valuable insights and stimulated substantial follow-up research, important limitations of this approach have remained largely unaddressed. In this paper, we highlight a crucial discrepancy between theory and practice of IDs in neu...

metadata.comment: Challenges intrinsic-dimension estimators for neural representations, showing common estimators fail to track true representation dimension and reframing what ID results mean.

title: Knowledge Capsules: Structured Nonparametric Memory Units for LLMs
summary: Large language models (LLMs) encode knowledge in parametric weights, making it costly to update or extend without retraining. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) mitigates this limitation by appending retrieved text to the input, but operates purely through context expansion, where external knowledge competes as tokens within the attention mechanism. As a result, its influence is indirect and often unstable, particularly in long context and multi hop reasoning scenarios. We propose Knowledge Capsules, structured nonparametric memory units that represent normalized relational knowledge and...
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Proposes structured nonparametric memory units that inject external key-values directly into attention instead of using text-based retrieval context.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

title: Convergent Evolution: How Different Language Models Learn Similar Number Representations
summary: Language models trained on natural text learn to represent numbers using periodic features with dominant periods at $T=2, 5, 10$. In this paper, we identify a two-tiered hierarchy of these features: while Transformers, Linear RNNs, LSTMs, and classical word embeddings trained in different ways all learn features that have period-$T$ spikes in the Fourier domain, only some learn geometrically separable features that can be used to linearly classify a number mod-$T$. To explain this incongruity, we prove that Fourier domain sparsity is necessary but not sufficient for mod-$T$ geometric separa...

metadata.comment: Studies how number representations emerge across architectures, distinguishing Fourier periodicity from geometrically separable modular features and analyzing training conditions that produce them.

title: Are LLM Uncertainty and Correctness Encoded by the Same Features? A Functional Dissociation via Sparse Autoencoders
summary: Large language models can be uncertain yet correct, or confident yet wrong, raising the question of whether their output-level uncertainty and their actual correctness are driven by the same internal mechanisms or by distinct feature populations. We introduce a 2x2 framework that partitions model predictions along correctness and confidence axes, and uses sparse autoencoders to identify features associated with each dimension independently. Applying this to Llama-3.1-8B and Gemma-2-9B, we identify three feature populations that play fundamentally different functional roles. Pure uncertainty...

metadata.comment: Uses sparse autoencoders to functionally dissociate internal features tied to uncertainty, incorrectness, and their confounding in LLMs.

title: Continuous Semantic Caching for Low-Cost LLM Serving
summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) become increasingly popular, caching responses so that they can be reused by users with semantically similar queries has become a vital strategy for reducing inference costs and latency. Existing caching frameworks have proposed to decide which query responses to cache by assuming a finite, known universe of discrete queries and learning their serving costs and arrival probabilities. As LLMs' pool of users and queries expands, however, such an assumption becomes increasingly untenable: real-world LLM queries reside in an infinite, continuous embedding space....

metadata.comment: Develops the first continuous-space theory for semantic caching with dynamic epsilon-net discretization and regret guarantees.

title: Decentralized Machine Learning with Centralized Performance Guarantees via Gibbs Algorithms
summary: In this paper, it is shown, for the first time, that centralized performance is achievable in decentralized learning without sharing the local datasets. Specifically, when clients adopt an empirical risk minimization with relative-entropy regularization (ERM-RER) learning framework and a forward-backward communication between clients is established, it suffices to share the locally obtained Gibbs measures to achieve the same performance as that of a centralized ERM-RER with access to all the datasets. The core idea is that the Gibbs measure produced by client~$k$ is used, as reference measu...

metadata.comment: Shows decentralized ERM with Gibbs-measure passing can match centralized performance by sharing inductive bias instead of data.

title: On the Stability and Generalization of First-order Bilevel Minimax Optimization
summary: Bilevel optimization and bilevel minimax optimization have recently emerged as unifying frameworks for a range of machine-learning tasks, including hyperparameter optimization and reinforcement learning. The existing literature focuses on empirical efficiency and convergence guarantees, leaving a critical theoretical gap in understanding how well these algorithms generalize. To bridge this gap, we provide the first systematic generalization analysis for first-order gradient-based bilevel minimax solvers with lower-level minimax problems. Specifically, by leveraging algorithmic stability arg...

metadata.comment: Provides the first algorithmic-stability generalization bounds for first-order bilevel minimax solvers.

title: Temporal Difference Calibration in Sequential Tasks: Application to Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Recent advances in vision-language-action (VLA) models for robotics have highlighted the importance of reliable uncertainty quantification in sequential tasks. However, assessing and improving calibration in such settings remains mostly unexplored, especially when only partial trajectories are observed. In this work, we formulate sequential calibration for episodic tasks, where task-success confidence is produced along an episode, while success is determined at the end of it. We introduce a sequential extension of the Brier score and show that, for binary outcomes, its risk minimizer coinci...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Connects sequential calibration to temporal-difference value estimation, making policy value learning a principled uncertainty-calibration mechanism in VLA sequential tasks.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: Sheaf Neural Networks on SPD Manifolds: Second-Order Geometric Representation Learning
summary: Graph neural networks face two fundamental challenges rooted in the linear structure of Euclidean vector spaces: (1) Current architectures represent geometry through vectors (directions, gradients), yet many tasks require matrix-valued representations that capture relationships between directions-such as how atomic orientations covary in a molecule. These second-order representations are naturally captured by points on the symmetric positive definite matrices (SPD) manifold; (2) Standard message passing applies shared transformations across edges. Sheaf neural networks address this via edge...

metadata.comment: Extends sheaf neural networks to SPD manifolds and argues matrix-valued sheaf representations are strictly more expressive than Euclidean ones.

title: Onyx: Cost-Efficient Disk-Oblivious ANN Search
summary: Approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search in AI systems increasingly handles sensitive data on third-party infrastructure. Trusted execution environments (TEEs) offer protection, but cost-efficient deployments must rely on external SSDs, which leaks user queries through disk access patterns to the host. Oblivious RAM (ORAM) can hide these access patterns but at a high cost; when paired with existing disk-based ANN search techniques, it makes poor use of SSD resources, yielding high latency and poor cost-efficiency. The core challenge for efficient oblivious ANN search over SSDs is balancing...

metadata.comment: Co-design of ANN pruning and locality-aware ORAM changes the bandwidth/access-count tradeoff for secure large-scale retrieval systems.

title: EvoForest: A Novel Machine-Learning Paradigm via Open-Ended Evolution of Computational Graphs
summary: It proposes a genuinely different learning paradigm centered on open-ended evolution of computation graphs rather than standard parameter fitting.

metadata.comment: Open-ended evolution of reusable computational graphs proposes a nonstandard paradigm for discovering model structure beyond weight optimization.
metadata.spotlight_comment: It proposes a genuinely different learning paradigm centered on open-ended evolution of computation graphs rather than standard parameter fitting.

title: Prism: An Evolutionary Memory Substrate for Multi-Agent Open-Ended Discovery
summary: This proposes a fairly broad new design space for agent memory substrates with formal mechanisms rather than standard memory plumbing.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Presents an explicit multi-agent memory substrate with entropy-gated storage, causal memory graphs, VOI retrieval, and consolidation dynamics.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
metadata.spotlight_comment: This proposes a fairly broad new design space for agent memory substrates with formal mechanisms rather than standard memory plumbing.

title: Gauge-Equivariant Graph Neural Networks for Lattice Gauge Theories
summary: Embedding fully local non-Abelian gauge symmetry into graph message passing feels like a genuinely new architectural paradigm.

metadata.comment: Extends equivariant message passing from global symmetries to local gauge symmetries via gauge-covariant matrix-valued features.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Embedding fully local non-Abelian gauge symmetry into graph message passing feels like a genuinely new architectural paradigm.

title: Forage V2: Knowledge Evolution and Transfer in Autonomous Agent Organizations
summary: It proposes an unusually explicit organizational-memory paradigm for agent systems that could open a broader design direction beyond standard chat memory.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Builds an organizational memory architecture that accumulates transferable knowledge across runs and across model capabilities.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
metadata.spotlight_comment: It proposes an unusually explicit organizational-memory paradigm for agent systems that could open a broader design direction beyond standard chat memory.

title: Temporally Extended Mixture-of-Experts Models
summary: Broadly important today: Applies the options framework to MoE routing so experts persist across multiple tokens, sharply reducing expert-switching overhead for memory-efficient serving.

metadata.comment: Applies the options framework to MoE routing so experts persist across multiple tokens, sharply reducing expert-switching overhead for memory-efficient serving.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Applies the options framework to MoE routing so experts persist across multiple tokens, sharply reducing expert-switching overhead for memory-efficient serving.

title: Stream-CQSA: Avoiding Out-of-Memory in Attention Computation via Flexible Workload Scheduling
summary: Exact billion-token attention on a single GPU is broadly notable beyond the niche personalization criteria because it changes the practical long-context scaling discussion.

metadata.comment: Recasts exact attention as schedulable subproblems via cyclic quorum set decomposition, enabling exact long-context attention under strict memory budgets.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Exact billion-token attention on a single GPU is broadly notable beyond the niche personalization criteria because it changes the practical long-context scaling discussion.

title: Occupancy Reward Shaping: Improving Credit Assignment for Offline Goal-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning
summary: Broadly important today: Extracts goal-reaching geometry from learned occupancy world models to shape rewards without changing the optimal policy.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Extracts goal-reaching geometry from learned occupancy world models to shape rewards without changing the optimal policy.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Extracts goal-reaching geometry from learned occupancy world models to shape rewards without changing the optimal policy.

title: Expert Upcycling: Shifting the Compute-Efficient Frontier of Mixture-of-Experts
summary: Broadly important today: Introduces expert upcycling for MoE models, expanding expert count during continued pretraining to reduce compute while preserving sparse inference cost.

metadata.comment: Introduces expert upcycling for MoE models, expanding expert count during continued pretraining to reduce compute while preserving sparse inference cost.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Introduces expert upcycling for MoE models, expanding expert count during continued pretraining to reduce compute while preserving sparse inference cost.

title: The Origin of Edge of Stability
summary: Broadly important today: Explains why gradient descent is forced toward the edge-of-stability threshold 2/η from arbitrary initialization.

metadata.comment: Explains why gradient descent is forced toward the edge-of-stability threshold 2/η from arbitrary initialization.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Explains why gradient descent is forced toward the edge-of-stability threshold 2/η from arbitrary initialization.

title: Super Apriel: One Checkpoint, Many Speeds
summary: A one-checkpoint many-speed model with layerwise mixer selection is broadly important for current model architecture and serving discussions.

metadata.comment: A single supernet checkpoint supports per-layer mixer choices at serving time, exposing architecture-speed tradeoffs without reloading weights.
metadata.spotlight_comment: A one-checkpoint many-speed model with layerwise mixer selection is broadly important for current model architecture and serving discussions.
